name: Brain MRI Dementia Protocol
description: Dedicated brain MRI protocol for evaluation of dementia including Alzheimer disease and vascular dementia

findings.technique: Dementia protocol MRI including volumetric T1, T2, FLAIR, and coronal sequences optimized for hippocampal and cortical evaluation.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Hippocampi
sections.content: The bilateral hippocampi demonstrate [no atrophy / mild hippocampal atrophy / moderate to severe hippocampal atrophy]. No hippocampal sclerosis.

sections.name: Entorhinal Cortex
sections.content: The entorhinal cortex demonstrates [no atrophy / no thinning / no abnormal signal]. The medial temporal lobe is otherwise [normal / shows atrophy].

sections.name: Cortical Atrophy
sections.content: The cerebral cortex demonstrates [no diffuse cortical atrophy / diffuse cortical atrophy / focal cortical atrophy]. The pattern is [predominantly posterior / diffuse / focal].

sections.name: White Matter
sections.content: The white matter demonstrates [no ischemic change / mild white matter hyperintensities / moderate white matter disease / severe white matter ischemic changes]. The Fazekas score is [0 / 1 / 2 / 3].

sections.name: Lacunes and Microinfarcts
sections.content: There are [no lacunes / X lacunar infarcts] identified in the [basal ganglia / thalamus / white matter / brainstem]. No chronic microhemorrhages on SWI.

sections.name: Ventricles
sections.content: The lateral ventricles demonstrate [no dilatation / mild ventricular dilatation / moderate to severe ventricular dilatation] consistent with [age-appropriate / ex vacuo] hydrocephalus.

sections.name: Vascular
sections.content: The intracranial arteries demonstrate [no significant stenosis / atherosclerotic disease]. No flow-limiting stenosis identified.

sections.name: Mass and Other
sections.content: No intracranial mass, hemorrhage, or suspicious lesion identified.

impression:
1. MRI brain for dementia evaluation.
2. [No evidence of neurodegenerative process / Findings suggestive of Alzheimer-type neurodegeneration].
author: RadReport Templates
